svg.style: background: #ffffff; background-color: light-dark(#ffffff, #121212); color-scheme: light dark;

path.style: fill: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255)); stroke: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255));

rect.style: fill: light-dark(#ffffff, var(--ge-dark-color, #121212)); stroke: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255));

display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: "Helvetica"; color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;

span.style: font-family: Poppins, "Noto Color Emoji", sans-serif; font-size: 14px; text-align: start; white-space-collapse: preserve; background-color: light-dark(rgb(255, 255, 255), rgb(18, 18, 18)); color: light-dark(rgb(66, 66, 66), rgb(180, 180, 180));

a.style: box-sizing: border-box; font-family: Poppins, "Noto Color Emoji", sans-serif; text-decoration-line: none; background-color: light-dark(rgb(255, 255, 255), rgb(18, 18, 18)); outline: none; cursor: pointer; transition: color 0.3s; touch-action: manipulation; font-size: 14px; text-align: start; white-space-collapse: preserve; color: light-dark(rgb(24, 144, 255), rgb(40, 144, 239));

span.style: font-family: Poppins, "Noto Color Emoji", sans-serif; font-size: 14px; text-align: start; white-space-collapse: preserve; background-color: light-dark(rgb(255, 255, 255), rgb(18, 18, 18)); color: light-dark(rgb(66, 66, 66), rgb(180, 180, 180));

font.span:
| sudo gpg --dearmor -o /usr/share/keyrings/mongodb-server-7.0.gpg -> this cmd downloads the MongoDB's public GPG key, which in ASCII format, and then this "gpg --dearmor " Converts the ASCII key (.asc) into binary format (.gpg) and now this convert key is PERMANENTLY saved to the system keyrings directory(/usr/share/keyrings/), now because this is PERMANENTLY saved here, u don't have to run this cmd again and again
SO if u visit this link: -

a.style: box-sizing: border-box; font-family: Poppins, "Noto Color Emoji", sans-serif; text-decoration-line: none; background-color: light-dark(rgb(255, 255, 255), rgb(18, 18, 18)); outline: none; cursor: pointer; transition: color 0.3s; touch-action: manipulation; font-size: 14px; text-align: start; white-space-collapse: preserve; color: light-dark(rgb(24, 144, 255), rgb(40, 144, 239));

span.style: font-family: Poppins, "Noto Color Emoji", sans-serif; font-size: 14px; text-align: start; white-space-collapse: preserve; background-color: light-dark(rgb(255, 255, 255), rgb(18, 18, 18)); color: light-dark(rgb(66, 66, 66), rgb(180, 180, 180));

font.span: in browser u will see many different GPG keys for different MongoDB versions/components in .asc and .pub format, so here .asc are for APT on debenian based destros , this is what we use and .pub are for RPM/YUM on RHEL, CentOS, Fedora. And version means different mongbdb repository versions have different key versions

div.span:
jammy/mongodb-org/7.0 multiverse" | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/mongodb-org-7.0.list -> this cmd just add the mongodb apt reposity url with the GPG key path to this path "/etc/apt/sources.list.d/mongodb-org-7.0.list'
IMP: - if visit this link: -

a.style: box-sizing: border-box; font-family: Poppins, "Noto Color Emoji", sans-serif; text-decoration-line: none; background-color: light-dark(rgb(255, 255, 255), rgb(18, 18, 18)); outline: none; cursor: pointer; transition: color 0.3s; touch-action: manipulation; font-size: 14px; text-align: start; white-space-collapse: preserve; color: light-dark(rgb(24, 144, 255), rgb(40, 144, 239));

span.style: color: light-dark(rgb(66, 66, 66), rgb(180, 180, 180)); font-family: Poppins, "Noto Color Emoji", sans-serif; font-size: 14px; text-align: start; white-space-collapse: preserve; background-color: light-dark(rgb(255, 255, 255), rgb(18, 18, 18));

div.span:
in browser, u will see many Ubuntu version codenames (like bionic, focal, jammy, noble etc..) there are all separate package builds for each Ubuntu version release by mongodb.
SO here we are using jammy which was release for Ubuntu 22.04, and if u r using Ubuntu 24.04 then u can also download noble version instead of jammy. Now this will PERMENENTLY add the mongodb url to this /etc/apt/sources.list.d/mongodb-org-7.0.list file, thatswhy now u don't need to run this cmd again and again

div.span: 3. sudo apt update: - this cmd will now downlaod the metadata(like package name, version, dependencies, file paths, maintainer, checksums, etc...) of all the urls that we have added in this directory "/etc/apt/sources.list.d/' with .list file . So incase of mongodb this url("

a.style: box-sizing: border-box; font-family: Poppins, "Noto Color Emoji", sans-serif; text-decoration-line: none; background-color: light-dark(rgb(255, 255, 255), rgb(18, 18, 18)); outline: none; cursor: pointer; transition: color 0.3s; touch-action: manipulation; font-size: 14px; text-align: start; white-space-collapse: preserve; color: light-dark(rgb(24, 144, 255), rgb(40, 144, 239));

div.span: jammy/mongodb-org/7.0 ") contain all mongodb repository like mongodb-server, mongodb-shell, mongodb-tools etc.., now once all these repository meta data have been add into the apt repository temporary cache, we can install these package with apt package manager now like this : - sudo apt install -y mongodb-org-server mongodb-mongosh mongodb-database-tools

switch.text: 3. sudo apt update: - this cmd will now downlaod the metadata(like package name, version, dependencies, file paths, maintainer, checksums, et...

div.span: Now if u r wondering why do we need different package for different ubuntu version, then the answer is: - Because packages are compiled against specific system libraries (glibc, openssl, etc.) that differ between Ubuntu versions. A binary built for jammy may not work on focal due to different library versions.
